LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANDRE LOPEZ'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON OCTOBER 23, 2017 IS GRANTED. MR. LOPEZ PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW EXCUSABLE NEGLECT AND GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. MR. LOPEZ MUST FILE A RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 2, 2018. THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED ON THIS MOTION REFUTES MR. LOPEZ'S ARGUMENT THAT HE WAS NOT SERVED WITH AN ARBITRATION NOTICE PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT BY PLAINTIFF WANG & CHANG. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Andre M. Lopez'S Motion To Set Aside Default, Alternatively For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Andre Lopez In Support Thereof; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Jan-23-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andre Lopez 650-370-3060 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested.

DEFENDANT ANDRE LOPEZ's Motion To Set Aside Default, Alternatively For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action.

Defendant Andre Lopez's motion to set aside the default entered against him on October 23, 2017 is granted. Mr. Lopez presented sufficient evidence to show excusable neglect and granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. Mr. Lopez must file a response to the complaint no later than February 2, 2018. The evidence submitted on this motion refutes Mr. Lopez's argument that he was not served with an arbitration notice prior to the filing of the complaint by plaintiff Wang & Chang.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)
